Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy. The company that 'owns' a university's career pipeline owns 10 years of talent flow.

The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.

Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now. In a world of AI-generated everything, the only reliable signal is direct interaction over time.
Ivor suggests Handshake as perfect partner: They provide college job boards to universities for free. Already have annotation/labeling business. V confirms Handshake is 'actual perfect acquirer'.

Original hypothesis VALIDATED: conversational modality is the correct way to capture human data. Strategic gap identified: going B2C to job seekers under duress was problematic.
V's pitch framework: 'Subtracting cognitive effort' from resume review. Everything framed as 'why say yes / why say no'. High-signal, low-volume approach.

Whoever builds the 'career OS' for a generation of college students and maintains that relationship for a decade controls the most valuable dataset in the labor market—one that can't be faked because it's built on years of authentic interaction. This is Careerspan's asymmetric bet.
